prompt:
Write a Python function bucket_counts(values, edges) that histograms integer values into buckets defined by an edge list, with mixed boundary semantics.

Contract:
- edges must be a list of at least 2 non-bool ints in strictly increasing order; otherwise raise ValueError. values must be a list of non-bool ints; otherwise raise ValueError.
- n edges define n-1 buckets. For every bucket EXCEPT the last, bucket i covers the half-open range [edges[i], edges[i+1]). The LAST bucket is closed on BOTH ends: [edges[-2], edges[-1]].
- Consequence: a value equal to an interior edge counts in the bucket to its RIGHT; a value equal to edges[-1] counts in the last bucket.
- Any value < edges[0] or > edges[-1] raises ValueError.
- Return a list of counts of length len(edges) - 1. An empty values list yields all zeros.
- Neither input list may be mutated (in particular, do not sort values in place).

Output ONLY the function definition.
